messages.content:
You are a hardware engineering assistant. Target board:
```json
{"id":"raspberry-pi-pico","name":"Raspberry Pi Pico","mcu":"RP2040"}
```
Respond with working code and a brief explanation.
Drive a 24-LED NeoPixel ring on GP15 with a slow rainbow.

You are a hardware engineering assistant. Target board:
```json
{"id":"adafruit-feather-esp32-s3","name":"Adafruit Feather ESP32-S3","mcu":"ESP32-S3","special":["NeoPixel on GPIO33 (power gate GPIO21)"]}
```
Respond with working code and a brief explanation.
Color-wipe the onboard NeoPixel through R, G, B with a 250 ms hold each, then loop.

You are a hardware engineering assistant. Target board:
```json
{"id":"esp32-s3-devkitc-1","name":"ESP32-S3 DevKitC-1","mcu":"ESP32-S3"}
```
Respond with working code and a brief explanation.
60 LED strip on GPIO5: a single white pixel chases at 30 fps from end to end and back.

You are a hardware engineering assistant. Target board:
```json
{"id":"adafruit-feather-nrf52840-sense","name":"Adafruit Feather nRF52840 Sense","mcu":"nRF52840","special":["NeoPixel on D8"]}
```
Respond with working code and a brief explanation.
Pulse the onboard NeoPixel green with a sine-wave brightness, 2 second period.

You are a hardware engineering assistant. Target board:
```json
{"id":"raspberry-pi-pico","name":"Raspberry Pi Pico","mcu":"RP2040"}
```
Respond with working code and a brief explanation.
144 LED strip on GP14: theater-chase white pixels every 3 LEDs, advancing every 80 ms.

You are a hardware engineering assistant. Target board:
```json
{"id":"esp32-s3-devkitc-1","name":"ESP32-S3 DevKitC-1","mcu":"ESP32-S3"}
```
Respond with working code and a brief explanation.
30 LED strip on GPIO5 acts as a VU meter from analogRead on GPIO1; full red at peak.

You are a hardware engineering assistant. Target board:
```json
{"id":"seeed-xiao-esp32-c3","name":"Seeed XIAO ESP32-C3","mcu":"ESP32-C3"}
```
Respond with working code and a brief explanation.
8 LED strip on D2 (GPIO4): sparkle effect, two random pixels lit white per frame at 20 fps.

You are a hardware engineering assistant. Target board:
```json
{"id":"raspberry-pi-pico","name":"Raspberry Pi Pico","mcu":"RP2040"}
```
Respond with working code and a brief explanation.
NeoPixel ring on GP6 changes color based on a button on GP15: red, green, blue, off, then loops.

You are a hardware engineering assistant. Target board:
```json
{"id":"esp32-s3-devkitc-1","name":"ESP32-S3 DevKitC-1","mcu":"ESP32-S3"}
```
Respond with working code and a brief explanation.
60 LED strip on GPIO5 with a fire effect: warm red/orange palette, gentle flicker.

You are a hardware engineering assistant. Target board:
```json
{"id":"adafruit-feather-esp32-s3","name":"Adafruit Feather ESP32-S3","mcu":"ESP32-S3"}
```
Respond with working code and a brief explanation.
Drive a 16x16 NeoPixel matrix on GPIO5 using Adafruit_NeoMatrix; scroll 'hello' in red.
